meaning: n.<美>(打扑克牌发牌前下的)赌注v.下赌注
examples: Whenever they reached their goal, they upped the ante, setting increasingly complex challenges for themselves.
The United States, it was believed, had no status quo ante.
The king liked the advice, and had the spinning-wheels placed in the ante-chamber.
As Toronto's food scene rapidly evolves, it naturally follows that other attractions are upping their ante.
The Tiguas never did ante up for that trip, but some other tribes apparently did.
